method_atom: A statistical framework for the abundance and spatial clustering of rare, high-threshold structures that condense out of a random fluctuation field, computing their number counts, second-order power spectrum, and angular two-point correlation function as functions of the field's underlying probability distribution (a Gaussian baseline versus a skewed chi-squared-with-one-degree-of-freedom alternative) and of the field's temporal growth law. The framework is used to test, self-consistently, which combination of distribution shape and growth history reproduces an observed level of fluctuation power.
object_atom: Galaxy clusters and the Sunyaev-Zeldovich imprint they leave on cosmic microwave background anisotropy in a Lambda-CDM universe characterized by the mass-variance parameter sigma_8, with the two alternative scenarios being non-Gaussian primordial density fluctuations and an early dark energy component.
brief_reason: The threshold-exceedance abundance/power-spectrum/correlation machinery for rare peaks of a random field under varying distribution shape and growth history is the transferable method, while the galaxy-cluster/S-Z/CMB cosmological setting is the domain-specific object.
